url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation. Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page. Each pick's competitor section includes 4-6 suggested queries + 4-6 known specialist-firm-class targets (e.g. BDO inbound services / Buzzacott UK inbound / RSM new arrival / Saffery Champness inbound / Mishcon de Reya private client for B7; RPC tax disputes / Pinsent Masons tax litigation / Crowe UK / Mercer Tax for B8 + B9; Saffery Champness ATED desk / Knight Frank Tax / Mercer Tax / Cobalt Tax / Crowe UK for B10; BDO ATED desk / Mercer Tax / Cobalt Tax / Ross Martin Group / Crowe / Saffery for B11; Mercer Tax / Cobalt Tax / Saffery / BDO ATED / ETC Tax / Crowe for B12).
url_verification_summary.stage2_webfetch_strategy: Stage 2 surfaced authority URLs from Stage 1 seed citations + cross-referenced §16 (DTA framing) + §17 (Wave 2 expat) + §17.3 (s.10A) + §17.4 (NRCGT) + §17.5 (NRL turn-off) + §17.6 (domicile reform) + §17.8 (FIG operational) + §17.9 (TRF) + §17.10 (CGT rebasing election + condition-3 UK-situs exclusion) + §18 (ATED Wave 3 full) + §18.1 (2026/27 + 2025/26 bands) + §18.2 (chargeable persons) + §18.3 (relief catalogue) + §18.4 (return mechanics + 5-yearly revaluation + PRBC + late-filing penalty schedule) + §18.5 (ATED-CGT abolition) + §18.6 (RoE) + §18.7 (OTM campaign) + §18.8 (do-not-write list) + §21.A (CT 3-figure + s.18N(3)) + §21.5 (CIHC depth) + §22.X (IHT LTR + tail-period + Sch A1 + s.48 / s.48ZA reframe + s.267 omission) + §27 (HMRC enquiry + Sch 24 / Sch 55 / Sch 56 + Perrin + Martland + 30-day window + statutory review) + §27.2 (Sch 55 paras 17-18 disclosure mitigation + F-5 Sch 24 no-12-month-qualifier lock) + §27.3 (Sch 56 late payment) + §27.4 (Perrin four-stage verbatim) + §27.5 (Martland three-stage) + §27.9 (do-not-write list verbatim) house position locks; per-page legislation.gov.uk + gov.uk + HMRC manual + BAILII verification deferred to RUN session under §16.35 per-write discipline. F-5 lock (Sch 24 para 10 NO 12-month qualifier on careless-unprompted floor; 12-month qualifier exists in Sch 41 para 13 only) carried verbatim where relevant (most directly engages B9 ATED penalties + appeals catalogue + B11 / B12 ATED relief / clawback briefs at penalty-exposure cross-reference). ITTOIA 2005 s.396B TAAR cite-correctness per Wave 6 §16.40 lock (NOT ITA 2007 s.396B) flagged for B5 ATED pros-cons but not directly bitten in B-B2 picks. Wave 6 §21.4 + §22 + §15.4 enacted-Act drift catches carried (FA 2026 s.4(1)(b) substitution → 35.75% s.455 dividend upper rate; NRB freeze to 5 April 2031; AIM 50% sub-tier separate). 1 April 2027 next 5-yearly ATED revaluation date (HP §18.4 verified gov.uk 2026-05-22) is LOAD-BEARING for B10 ATED rates.

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified FA 2013 s.99 + s.101 ATED indexation mechanism (2026-05-27) / FA 2013 s.133 + s.135 + s.136 + s.139 + s.159 + s.161 + s.163 ATED relief + clawback + return obligations (2026-05-27) / FA 2009 Sch 55 para 23 reasonable excuse VERBATIM + para 16 special reduction VERBATIM + Table at para 1 items 11A + 11B coverage (2026-05-27) / TCGA 1992 s.10A per HMRC CG26540 (2026-05-22) / FA 2025 s.37 FIG / FA 2025 s.41 + Sch 10 TRF / FA 2025 s.42 + Sch 11 CGT rebasing election / IHTA 1984 s.6A LTR + Sch A1 / FA 1995 Sch 23 + SI 1995/2902 NRL / Perrin v HMRC [2018] UKUT 156 (TCC) four-stage framework / Martland v HMRC [2018] UKUT 178 (TCC) three-stage late-appeal at seed time. Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §16 + §17 (full) + §17.8 + §17.9 + §17.10 + §18 + §18.1 (verified gov.uk figures) + §18.4 (penalty schedule verbatim) + §21.A + §21.5 (CIHC + s.18N(3)) + §22.X (LTR + Sch A1) + §27 (Perrin + Martland + 30-day + statutory review) + §27.4 + §27.5 + §27.9 locks; no new drift catches at Stage 2 review.

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 6 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch were unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers (matching M3-B-B1 D-2 + M3-A-B1 D-2 + M3-A-B2 D-2 patterns). Each extended brief landed at +227 to +277 lines of additional content (worked examples 4-6 per pick + 12-14 FAQs + universal rules + 19-step workflow + Stage 1 seed work log appendix). This pattern across A-B1, A-B2, A-B4, B-B1, B-B2, C-B1, C-B2 confirms Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_INBOUND_OUTBOUND_PAIR_DISCIPLINE — pick 1 (arriving-in-the-uk, B7) is the structural mirror of B6 (are-you-leaving-the-uk-permanently, extended in M3-B-B1 prior batch). Stage 2 anti-templating discipline maintained: B7 opens with FIG-vs-rebasing-vs-LTR triangulation (inbound entry-points first); B6 opens with the s.10A 5-year line (outbound exit-points first). Both share §17.3 + §17.8 + §17.9 + §17.10 + §22.X HP locks but address direction-distinct framing. The matched-pair structure is a load-bearing site architecture: cross-link bidirectional between B7 (inbound mirror) + B6 (outbound mirror) + B19 (short-term variant) + B1/B2/B3 (specific-state DTA) is mandatory at RUN time.

D-4 STAGE2_ATED_CLUSTER_DISCIPLINE — picks 2 (B8 ATED late-filing two-routes appeal) + 3 (B9 ATED penalties + appeals catalogue) + 4 (B10 ATED rates) + 5 (B11 ATED relief clawback s.135) + 6 (B12 ATED related-persons + market rent) form a 5-page ATED cluster all anchored on §18 (full Wave 3 ATED extension) + §27 (enquiry / appeals architecture) + §21.A + §21.5 (CIHC). Cluster-level differentiation discipline maintained at Stage 2: B8 is the operational two-routes appeal page (deep-dive on para 23 reasonable excuse + para 16 special reduction); B9 is the architectural catalogue across Sch 55 + Sch 56 + Sch 24 + appeal mechanics (cross-links to B8 for the routes deep-dive); B10 is the rates-and-mechanism page (s.99 + s.101 indexation + 1 April 2027 revaluation + PRBC + mixed-use); B11 is the s.135 clawback look-forward + look-back deep-dive (with B12 cross-link); B12 is the related-persons + market-rent decision-page (with B11 cross-link for the clawback). Each pick's anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk + structurally-distinct opening + differentiating worked-example archetypes. RUN session pairing of B8 + B9 + B10 + B11 + B12 should hold the line on per-page distinctness.

D-5 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 6 picks. No mid-batch pause; no Q-N raised; no blocker. Wall-clock matches the M3-A-B1 D-4 + M3-A-B2 D-5 + M3-B-B1 D-5 patterns (~2-2.5 hours for 6 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker).

D-6 STAGE2_CROSS_PICK_LTR_CONSISTENCY — pick 1 (B7 arriving-in-uk) carries Example 5 Sir Henderson HNW inbound LTR clock walkthrough (US citizen; year 1 of UK residence; LTR trigger at year 10; pre-LTR estate-planning window). This complements B6 (M3-B-B1) Example 4 LTR tail-period table (Chen / Khan / Roberts at 6 / 13 / 19 prior UK-resident years → no-LTR / 3-year tail / 9-year tail) which walks the OUTBOUND scaling. Site-wide LTR discipline anchor IHTA 1984 ss.6A-6C + s.6B under-20 variation + ss.267ZC-267ZF election framework + Sch A1 look-through + s.267 omission + s.48 / s.48ZA reframe should remain internally consistent at RUN time across B7 + B6 + B2 + B3 + B5 (M3-B-B1 stage 2 extensions) + this batch's pick 1.

D-7 STAGE2_ATED_S136_CATALOGUE_DISCIPLINE — picks 5 (B11) + 6 (B12) both depend on the FA 2013 s.136 eight-category non-qualifying-individual catalogue. The catalogue is LOAD-BEARING — RUN session must reproduce each category verbatim from legislation.gov.uk at write per §16.35. Categories (d) settlors of trusts + (e) relatives + (f) spouses of relatives are the most-under-stated by competitor commentary. Multi-hop family (spouse of relative of connected person) IS caught. The CIHC parallel test at CTA 2010 s.18N(3) per §21.5 has a similar-but-not-identical catalogue (connected persons + spouses + relatives + relatives' spouses); sessions must not conflate. B11 covers the s.135 clawback mechanic; B12 covers the related-persons-decision frame. Both cite both tests separately.

D-8 STAGE2_ATED_RATES_INDEXATION_MECHANISM — pick 4 (B10) is structurally the rates-and-mechanism canonical landing. Stage 2 brief instructs explicit two-table comparison (2026/27 + 2025/26 with year-on-year delta) + the s.99 + s.101 indexation walk (FA 2013 s.99(4) original-enacted base + s.101 CPI mechanism + £50 round-down + Treasury order timing + cumulative 13-year uplift). The 1 April 2027 next-revaluation date is LOAD-BEARING — owners holding properties near band boundaries (£500k / £1m / £2m / £5m / £10m / £20m) should plan for the 1 April 2027 valuation reset NOW. RUN session must surface this proactively per HP §18.4 verified gov.uk 2026-05-22.

D-9 STAGE2_PENALTY_ARCHITECTURE_DISCIPLINE — picks 2 (B8) + 3 (B9) together establish the canonical ATED penalty + appeal architecture: B8 is the operational two-routes deep-dive (Sch 55 para 23 + para 16 + Perrin v HMRC + Martland v HMRC + 30-day TMA 1970 s.31A); B9 is the architectural overview across all three regimes (Sch 55 late filing + Sch 56 late payment + Sch 24 inaccuracy) + the appeal mechanics + the OTM-letter context + the cumulative-multi-year exposure. The split-brief design avoids cannibalisation while giving the reader two distinct entry points (operational vs catalogue). Cross-link bidirectional between B8 + B9 is mandatory. F-5 lock per §27.2 (Sch 24 para 10 careless-unprompted-within-12-months 0% floor has NO 12-month qualifier — that qualifier exists in Sch 41 para 13 only) carries verbatim across both B8 + B9 disclosure-mitigation discussion.
anti_templating_check: Six briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow, Quality bar) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk: B7 arriving-in-uk (vs B6 outbound mirror + B19 short-term variant + B1/B2/B3 specific-state DTA + FIG-vs-rebasing-vs-LTR triangulation anchor); B8 ated-late-filing-appeal (vs B9 broader catalogue + B16 error-mechanics + B4 OTM-response + two-routes-do-not-collapse anchor); B9 ated-penalties-and-appeals (vs B8 operational two-routes + B16 + B4 + three-regimes architectural-completeness anchor); B10 ated-rates (vs B5 strategic + B14 valuation-date deep-dive + B8/B9 penalty + B11/B12 relief + verified 2026/27 + indexation mechanism anchor); B11 ated-relief-clawback (vs B12 related-persons-decision + B5 strategic + B8/B9 penalty + B13 ACA returns + look-forward 3-period-shock anchor); B12 ated-related-persons-market-rent (vs B11 clawback mechanic + B5 strategic + B8/B9 + market-rent-myth-refusal anchor). No same-sentence drift detected across the set; each worked-example persona surname differs per pick (B7 arriving: Khoury/Olsson/Hayek/Henderson; B8 appeal: Topaz Holdings/Cordoba Holdings Cayman/Acer Property Partners LLP; B9 penalties: Iberic Holdings/Cordova Cayman/Sapphire Estates Cayman; B10 rates: Trevor Holdings/Olivetti Holdings/Saffron Estates Cayman; B11 clawback: Rosewood/Ironwood Cayman/Sycamore/Pomegranate + Ms Pomegranate + Jacob; B12 related-persons: Honeysuckle + Mrs Honeysuckle + Tom / Magnolia + Mr+Mrs Magnolia / Walnut + Mr Walnut + Susan Ferris / Olive + Mr+Mrs Olive). No surname collisions with prior M3-B-B1 batch personas (Carter/Ellis/Chen/Khan/Roberts/Marquez/Patel for B6; Singh-Patel/Walker/Sapling/Ferguson/Cordero for B5; Quayle/Crosby/Kissack/Cregeen for B1; Hernández/Vázquez/Brown/Wright for B2; Iyengar/Mehta/Singh/Chowdhury/Reddy/Vaidyanathan for B3; Bridge/Magnolia Investments Limited for B4 — note: 'Magnolia' surname appears in B12 pick 6 as a different persona/structure (Magnolia Holdings Limited + Mr+Mrs Magnolia — UK ltd-co spouse-as-company-secretary structure) vs M3-B-B1 B4's 'Magnolia Investments Limited' (different context — overseas-corporate OTM letter response). RUN session should review for inadvertent surname collisions; the structures are different enough that confusion is unlikely but cross-batch persona discipline could be tightened.
